key_insight: PIBLS turns PDE solving into a direct least-squares problem, giving a backpropagation-free framework with proven universal approximation for PDEs.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, mesh-free alternative to PINNs that can support real-time simulation and design optimization in scientific and engineering workflows.
method: Reformulate physics-informed PDE learning as least-squares optimization
Use a Broad Learning System architecture instead of backpropagation
Extend the method with an improved algorithm for nonlinear PDEs and prove universal approximation
evidence_signal: Experiments on linear and nonlinear PDEs show PIBLS is 1 to 3 orders of magnitude faster than conventional PINNs while achieving higher solution accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the stability of the least-squares formulation and the scalability of the broad-feature representation for harder or larger PDE systems.
paper_title: Learning universal approximations for partial differential equations with Physics-Informed Broad Learning System
